rules.reason: Translated z-score conditions using Bollinger Bands percentile (bb_pct_b). Entry occurs when price deviates beyond 1 standard deviation (bb_pct_b < 0.0 for long, > 1.0 for short). Exit when price returns to moving average (bb_pct_b ≈ 0.5 or close crosses sma_20). Used sma_20 as the moving average reference point for mean reversion. bb_pct_b directly represents normalized distance from moving average in standard deviations.

Converted short_window=50 to sma_50 and long_window=200 to sma_200. Entry requires SMA 50 to cross above SMA 200, price above both MAs, and position OUT state (implicit in entry logic). Exit triggered when SMA 200 crosses above SMA 50 (reversal signal). CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction resolved to LONG based on entry conditions.

Mapped short_window=10 to sma_10 and long_window=50 to sma_50. Entry requires both crossover detection and confirmation that short SMA is above long SMA. Exit triggers on bearish crossover. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but structured as LONG entries with corresponding exits; SHORT signals would use inverse logic on the same crossover conditions.

Signal is context-dependent (both long and short entries included). Used rsi_14 as proxy for momentum indicator. Volume threshold compared to vol_ratio_20 (volume relative to 20-period MA). Minimum trend bars requirement (3-5 bars) handled by requiring price extension beyond sma_5. Divergence detection not directly available in indicator set, so omitted from entry conditions. Exit momentum threshold (50) applied to RSI crosses. Hold period set to 10 days as reasonable default for momentum trade.

Signal requires context-dependent directional bias. Mapped 'swing high/low breaks' to close > prev_high (long) and close < prev_low (short). Volume increase >50% mapped to vol_ratio_20 > 1.5 (50% above baseline). Price acceleration (>0.75 ATR) mapped directly to atr_14 > 0.75. Directional bias (3+ period trend) approximated with ADX_14 > 25 indicating established trend strength. Exit conditions use 3-period swing reversal as close crossing previous swing extremes.

Signal is context-dependent (long/short based on price direction). Entry requires: price breakout from previous bar + ATR expansion (atr_14 > sma_20 approximates ATR > 20-period MA of ATR) + RSI directional confirmation + volume above 40th percentile (vol_ratio_20 > 0.40). Exit triggers on bar reversal or volatility contraction. Position size multiplier (1.5x) noted but not encoded in JSON. Rate-of-change confirmation mapped to RSI thresholds as ROC is not in allowed indicators.
DSL translation of BULKOWSKI_DRY_0
_relaxation_notes: Removed regime filter (ADX already checks trend)

rules.reason: Signal uses abstract 'momentum signal' concept. Mapped to MACD crossovers as a standard momentum indicator: positive momentum = MACD > signal line, negative/reversal = MACD < signal line. Exit conditions include both momentum reversal and position loss (2% threshold as reasonable proxy for loss detection). Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to support both long and short momentum trades. Stop loss mechanism is implicit in the momentum reversal exit condition rather than a fixed percentage.

Signal requires regime detection (momentum vs mean-reversion). Used ADX_14 as primary trend/momentum identifier: ADX > 25 indicates strong trend (momentum regime, apply tight stops); ADX < 20 indicates weak trend/ranging (mean-reversion regime, relax stops). Fundamental deterioration detection not directly available; use price behavior relative to moving averages as proxy. Entry conditions detect trend direction; exit conditions detect regime shift to mean-reversion.

Selling climax modeled as high volume spike with large lower wick relative to body. Volatility compression detected via Donchian bands narrowing (implicit in dc_upper/dc_lower usage). Breakout above consolidation high using dc_upper on closing basis. Volume confirmation via vol_ratio_20. Exit on close below consolidation low (dc_lower). Resistance levels would require external pivot data or prior price highs not available in standard indicators.

ADX_14 > 25 confirms established trend. Price action relative to previous bar confirms pullback and continuation direction. Stop loss placed at recent pullback extreme (prev_high/prev_low). Exit triggers on trend structure break (close beyond pullback) or ADX collapse indicating trend weakness.

Parallel trend channels approximated using Bollinger Bands (bb_upper/bb_lower as channel boundaries) and SMA_20 (trend direction confirmation). Entry conditions require price within channel and trend confirmation via ADX. Exit conditions trigger on boundary breach (upper/lower band) or trend structure break (crossing SMA in opposite direction). Logic applies to both uptrend (long) and downtrend (short) contexts as specified. Position sizing/defensive approach implicit in exit-at-boundary rules.

Translated 'higher highs and higher lows' (uptrend) and 'lower lows and lower highs' (downtrend) into comparable bar logic. Used ADX_14 > 25 as proxy for 'momentum shift from prior regime' to confirm trend strength. Pattern violation (break of prior swing) serves as exit. Original thresholds marked [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] could not be precisely quantified from source text, so conservative defaults applied (ADX confirmation, 1-bar swing breaks). Entry conditions use price_pos comparisons relative to previous bars to detect directional persistence.

Signal requires bar range expansion relative to recent average (volatility ratio). Mapped using 'range' (high-low) compared to atr_14 as proxy for volatility threshold. Used pivot as support/resistance level substitute. Volume confirmation uses hvol_20 (20-bar historical volatility). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both breakouts above (LONG) and below (SHORT) levels. Assumed 2% stop-loss and 20-day max hold as reasonable defaults for breakout trades.

Signal is context-dependent (pullbacks in both uptrends and downtrends). Entry conditions operationalize: (1) trend confirmation via ADX > 25, (2) price near MA (pullback into MA), (3) reduced bar range via ATR_14 < ATR_20 (current bars narrower than recent), (4) absence of countertrend momentum via RSI not in extreme zones. Exit conditions trigger on trend break (price crosses MA) or strong countertrend momentum (RSI extremes). Stop-loss and hold parameters are conservative defaults for pullback trades.

Converted MA alignment requirement into explicit ordering conditions: uptrend has 20MA > 50MA > 200MA with price above 20MA; downtrend has 20MA < 50MA < 200MA with price below 20MA. Exit conditions use price crossing through the 20MA as the primary exit trigger per the signal description. The signal's complexity regarding support/resistance behavior during partial retracements is captured by monitoring the 20MA as the primary exit level; stronger pullbacks to 50MA or 200MA would require additional exit conditions in practice but are approximated here.

Signal requires detection of: (1) established trend—proxied by ADX > 25; (2) gap in trend direction—not directly observable from OHLC data alone, requires external bar comparison; (3) retracement to significant level—approximated using pivot points and moving average (sma_20); (4) trend resumption—entry triggered when price crosses back through pivot/MA in trend direction. Exit uses simple MA crossover as proxy for trend reversal per AUTHOR_SILENT instruction. Entry tactics reference cannot be operationalized without additional context. Context-dependent direction requires separate LONG/SHORT condition branches for bidirectional trend trading.

Used Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower, dc_middle) to represent range boundaries. Entry long when price crosses above lower boundary, entry short when price crosses below upper boundary. Exit conditions use middle line as reversal point per AUTHOR_SILENT guidance. Range duration approximated as 5 days for hold period. The signal is context-dependent: long at lower boundary, short at upper boundary.

Multi-month low support approximated using s2 pivot support level. Entry requires: price below s2 (multi-month low proxy), price below 20MA (bearish confirmation), and price below pivot (failed monthly pivot crosses). Exit on reversal above s2. AUTHOR_SILENT exit conditions addressed with reasonable reversal stop. Stop-loss set to 3% to account for volatility in pharma sector context.

Signal uses MA line slope detection via comparison with previous bar. Used sma_50 as default MA since author did not specify period. MA slope upwards detected by sma_50 > prev_close (price direction proxy). Bi-directional signal with both LONG and SHORT entry/exit conditions. Context-dependent direction shifts bias based on MA slope and price crossover.

Giant candle size is inferred from body size relative to ATR. Negligible tails are expressed as upper_wick and lower_wick both being smaller than body. Green/red determined by close vs open relationship. Exit conditions use reversal signal (opposite color candle) as author was silent. Direction is context-dependent: long on green giant candles with negligible tails, short on red giant candles with negligible tails.

Signal describes: (1) uptrend condition proxied by close > sma_20; (2) tiny red candle = small body size relative to ATR and red close (close < prev_close); (3) red candle at top of previous green = positioned after higher previous close; (4) next candle takes out the high = current high > prev_high (breakout confirmation). Exit when trend breaks below 20-SMA. Stop loss set conservatively at 2% given small candle volatility pattern.

Signal translated as context-dependent with separate long and short entry/exit conditions. Long entries: open above pivot, pullback confirmed by close above pivot and above S1 support. Short entries: open below pivot, pullback confirmed by close below pivot and below R1 resistance. Exits target R2/S2 as primary profit targets. Stop-loss and holding period not specified by author; backtest engine should apply defaults. Conditions use pivot point levels (pivot, r1, r2, s1, s2) as core decision variables per signal specification.

Signal is context-dependent on trend direction. Entry conditions for uptrend: price moves above S1 or between pivot and S1. Entry conditions for downtrend: open above pivot, above CPR (approximated as pivot), or between CPR and R1. Exit for uptrend targets R1/R2 or higher; exit for downtrend is reversal below pivot. CPR (Central Pivot Range) is approximated using pivot level. Author provided minimal exit guidance (AUTHOR_SILENT), so exit conditions use reasonable reversal logic.

Signal uses generic SMA crossover without specified periods. Used sma_10 (shorter-term) and sma_50 (longer-term) as standard trend-following periods. Volume confirmation added using prev_volume comparison. Price close conditions use the shorter SMA (sma_10) as reference. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per original signal specification—entries and exits trigger in both directions based on crossover direction.

Signal parameters SMA1_period and SMA2_period were not specified in original source. Based on Figure 4-1 context (EUR/USD with two SMAs) and standard practice, mapped to sma_5 (fast) and sma_20 (slow). Original rule allows bidirectional trading: long on crosses_above, short on crosses_below, with reversal exits. Target regimes set to TRENDING variants as indicated.

Signal uses generic 'faster' and 'slower' MA without specific periods. Applied standard 5/20 SMA crossover as a reasonable default for trending strategies. The CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction generates both long and short signals based on crossover direction. Entry conditions include crossover confirmation plus price position above/below the slower MA. Exit triggered immediately on reversal crossover per 'exit at market on crossover signal'.

Signal uses rolling mean of log returns across multiple lookback periods (15, 30, 60, 120 days). Entry conditions based on sign of rolling mean: positive log_returns indicates uptrend (long), negative indicates downtrend (short). Exit when sign flips. The rolling mean calculation is implicit in the log_returns indicator evaluation across the specified lookback windows. Position sizing is equal-weighted across all four momentum horizons as specified.

CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction splits into separate entry_long and entry_short blocks. Long trade triggers on upward gap (open > prev_close), retracement below previous close (close < prev_close), and close above open. Short trade is the inverse for downward gaps. Exit conditions match the expected close direction relative to open for each side.

Mapped 1.5 standard deviations cluster definition to Bollinger Bands (which use 2 standard deviations by default, but represent similar volatility-based clustering). Entry triggers when price returns into the band range (crosses back into cluster). Exit triggers when price breaks out of the cluster boundary. Context-dependent direction handles both long entries (price crosses above lower band) and short entries (price crosses below upper band) within the ranging regime.

Used ADX_14 > 25 as trend confirmation signal (longer-term trend indicator). Used stoch_k for short-term overbought (>80) and oversold (<20) conditions. Entry triggers on first stochastic extreme after trend confirmation. Exit on stochastic mean reversion (crossing 50 level). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both long (overbought entry) and short (oversold entry) trades based on trend direction.

CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction mapped to both long and short entries with identical volatility filters. 40-day moving average signal interpreted as price crossing sma_40. Low volatility filter (0.25) applied as entry condition; high volatility filter (1.60) triggers exit. hvol_20 used as volatility metric (20-period historical volatility in decimal scale). Exit condition inverted from entry: exit when volatility rises above threshold, consistent with 'avoid entry when high volatility is present' logic.

Signal uses 'exponential smoothing trendline' which maps to EMA. Selected ema_20 as a reasonable intermediate-term exponential moving average for trend following. The signal is context-dependent (both long and short entries), so both entry_long/exit_long and entry_short/exit_short are populated with mirror conditions. Exit conditions are reversal-based: long exits when price crosses below the trendline, short exits when price crosses above it.

Mapped 'faster moving average' to ema_10 and 'slower moving average' to ema_50 based on typical Kaufman-style multi-timeframe trend logic. 'Long-term moving average conflict' interpreted as ema_200 crossing conditions for neutral/exit signals. Entry conditions require price above both fast/slow MAs with fast MA below slow MA (bullish setup), reversed for shorts. Open interest measurement cannot be translated without futures-specific indicators; excluded from JSON. Signal is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per source: buy on bullish MA alignment, sell on bearish alignment or long-term MA conflict.

Outside day pattern: high > prev_high AND low < prev_low. Outside close: close > prev_high (bullish) OR close < prev_low (bearish). Direction determined by close relative to prior day's range. Exit conditions inferred as reversal (close crossing back through prior day's close) since author provided no specific exit guidance. Pattern frequency warning noted but not encoded as exit condition.

Entry: prices turn up detected via close > prev_close AND close > sma_5 (5-day MA confirms uptrend). Exit: price closes below 5-day MA (reversal signal, since exit conditions were AUTHOR_SILENT). Short hold_days_max (5) reflects short-term applicability stated in parameters.

Signal uses moving_average_period of 40 days, mapped to sma_40. Price reference is close price. Direction is context-dependent: long entries on crosses above the MA, short entries on crosses below. Exit conditions are reversal crossovers (opposite of entry). Target regime set to TRENDING modes as per original specification.

Used MACD histogram (6-12-9 default) as proxy for 6-day momentum. Entry requires price reversal (MACD crossover) within established trend (price position relative to 40-day SMA for trend direction and 20-day SMA to confirm small reversal, not trend change). Hold window set to 3 days per 'about three days' parameter. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal specifies both long and short setups based on trend direction.

Strangle strategy: Entry requires underlying between strike prices (45-50 OTM range) with elevated volatility. Exit triggers at profitable breakout levels (>54 or <41) or total loss if expiration occurs between strikes. Strike prices (call=50, put=45) hardcoded into conditions. Max hold set to 31 days (January expiration). Stop loss set to 100% reflecting max loss on strangle premium. hvol_20 used as proxy for volatility regime confirmation.

Signal uses context-dependent direction based on trend confirmation. Entry long when price above both moving averages with strong ADX; entry short when price below both MAs with strong ADX. 'Top 30% of recent range' translated to price_pos_20 > 0.7 for longs and < 0.3 for shorts. 'Fixed profit target at 2x risk' implies 2% stop-loss for typical 1:2 risk-reward. Position scaling based on ADX strength is implemented via entry condition threshold.

Signal uses trend confirmation across multiple indicators. Entry conditions mapped to long/short contexts based on MA alignment. 'Top 40% of range' translated to price_pos_20 > 0.6 for long, < 0.4 for short. Trailing stop at 1.5x ATR approximated as 3% stop_loss_pct given typical ATR relationships. Position scaling logic noted but not directly encodable in rule structure—should be handled in position sizing module.

Signal uses context-dependent direction (long in uptrends, short in downtrends). Entry conditions use sma_10 and sma_20 as closest match to '10-period MA' and '20-period MA'. 'Close near recent highs' approximated using price_pos_20 (position in 20-period range). ADX threshold set at 20 as specified. Exit uses ADX < 18 threshold. Stop loss set to 1.0 ATR equivalent (1.0%). Position sizing logic (initial 3%, scale factor 1.5) should be implemented in backtest engine separately.

Signal uses CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction, so both entry_long and entry_short conditions are provided. ATR stop loss (1.5x ATR) is converted to stop_loss_pct (1.5) as a multiplier approximation. Confirmation of second consecutive bar is implicit in the RSI cross and close beyond MA conditions. Used vol_ratio_20 for volume confirmation against 1.5x multiple. RSI_14 used as closest match to signal's RSI reference.

Signal uses RSI crosses 50 OR momentum crosses zero. Momentum indicator not in allowed list, so translated primary RSI condition. Volume condition mapped to vol_ratio_20. One confirming bar represented by body direction matching trend. ATR-based stop loss set to 1.0x (1.0%). Price close beyond 10-period MA used as both entry trend confirmation and exit reversal signal.

Signal emphasizes trend change detection via MA and momentum crosses. Used sma_5 per lookback_period parameter and rsi_14 as closest match to rsi_threshold of 50. Stop loss set to 0.5x ATR using atr_14 with 0.5 multiplier converted to percentage equivalent (0.5%). Entry is context-dependent (LONG on uptrend, SHORT on downtrend). Volume confirmation explicitly not required per signal. Exit triggers on trend reversal (MA cross in opposite direction).

Signal implements bidirectional trend-following logic. Entry requires price crossing 20-period MA, ADX confirmation (>18), and directional confirmation via prior close comparison as proxy for '3 prior closes' requirement (exact multi-bar comparison not supported). Exit uses MA crossover reversal and ADX weakening (<15). ADX_14 used as the only available ADX indicator. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to enable both long and short entries based on trend direction.

Signal uses 10-period MA (mapped to sma_10) and ADX thresholds. Direction is context-dependent: entry_long triggers on uptrend confirmation, entry_short on downtrend confirmation. Exit conditions use OR logic to capture either MA crossover or ADX trend weakness. The 'confirmation_bars: 1' parameter is implicit in the close > prev_close / close < prev_close conditions.

Signal emphasizes waiting for 3+ indicator confirmation before entry to avoid premature entry. Implemented using ADX (trend strength), price vs SMA (direction), and MACD (momentum confirmation). ADX threshold of 25+ ensures trend initiation. Exit triggers on first reversal of MACD signal to enforce the 'any 2 of 3 indicators reverse' rule with conservative approach. The confirmation_bars_minimum of 2 bars is handled by backtest engine bar-by-bar processing.

Signal requires 2+ indicator confirmation of trend direction. Implemented using: (1) price direction (close vs prev_close), (2) trend strength via ADX_14 > 25 threshold, and (3) momentum confirmation via MACD cross. ADX > 25 indicates established trend. MACD crossover serves as primary reversal signal for exits. Support CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction with separate long/short entry conditions.

Signal emphasizes waiting for technical confirmation before entering with trend. Interpreted as: (1) price movement in intended direction (close above/below open for direction confirmation), and (2) at least one primary indicator confirms (price above/below SMA_20 as primary trend confirmation). Exit when confirming indicator reverses (price closes back through SMA_20). Used SMA_20 as accessible trend-following indicator. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal handles both long and short scenarios with same logic. Target regimes limited to TRENDING as signal specifies trend-confirmation methodology.

Signal uses context-dependent entry: LONG when price crosses above SMA20 with ADX>20, SHORT when price crosses below SMA20 with ADX>20. Exit on reversal (price closes opposite side of MA) OR ADX falls below 20. Stop loss set at 3% account risk as specified.

Signal is context-dependent (bidirectional). Entry triggers on MA crossover with ADX confirmation (>15). Exit on MA crossover in opposite direction OR ADX trend exhaustion (<15). Stop loss set to 4% account risk as specified. Confirmation bars set to 0 means immediate entry on signal. Using sma_10 (10-period MA) and adx_14 (standard ADX indicator) from allowed list.

Signal requires 2+ setup conditions with multitimeframe confirmation. Translated to: (1) ADX > 25 to confirm trend establishment, (2) Price vs SMA_20 for directional setup. Exit when setup invalidated (price crosses SMA). Multiframe confirmation approximated through ADX threshold. Context-dependent direction handled via parallel LONG/SHORT rule sets. Hold max set to 5 bars per 'significant movement' requirement.

Signal requires 'trend established over minimum 3 months' which cannot be directly encoded as a single condition; this should be enforced at backtest initialization via lookback_period_months=90. All other entry conditions (price > 100-SMA, SMA alignment, ADX > 25) are directly translatable. Exit conditions use standard trend reversal signals. Direction is LONG as the rule focuses on entering obvious uptrends.

Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to detect new multi-day extremes. Entry long: price tests new high then closes lower (reversal). Entry short: price tests new low then closes higher (reversal). Small body_pct indicates reversal exhaustion. Exit conditions trigger on continuation through previous bar extremes. Stop loss set at 2.5x ATR equivalent (2.5%) as specified. Hold max 5 bars per signal specification. Gap closure percentage (50% minimum) cannot be directly encoded without lookback indexing, so reversal structure detection via body_pct and close positioning against prev_close/prev_high/prev_low serves as proxy.

Signal uses retracement-after-breakout logic. 'New extreme' detected via price exceeding pivot levels. Retracement mapped to close not exceeding prev_high (long) or prev_low (short). Volume confirmation via vol_ratio_20. Stop-loss set to 1.5 ATR equivalent as percentage. Entry conditions require AND logic for confirmation; exit uses OR logic (first-to-touch). Retracement depth (38.2%) is implicit in the conditional structure rather than explicit calculation.

Climax day identified using Donchian breakout (new extreme); retracement zone defined using price_pos_20 (0-1 scale) between 50-78.6%; entry on reversal signal from zone (price crossing price_pos_20 threshold); exit at 3 ATR using true_range expansion; no filters applied per THARP principle (filter_count_allowed=0)
